title: Homeless School Transition Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Homeless School Transition Programs 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION MCKINNEY-VENTO HOMELESS EDUCATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps by connecting students to community resources and minimizes school disruptions. This help can include enrolling in school without documents or transportation to and from school. 255 Capitol Street NE Salem, OR 97310 Eligibility: Students who are at risk of becoming homeless or are currently homeless. This includes students who: -Not in the physical custody of a parent or guardiann (unaccompanied minors) -Lack of a fixed, regular, nighttime, adequate place of residence -Stay in shelters, motels, or campgrounds because they have nowhere else to go -Live in cars, parks, abandoned buildings, or bus/train stations -Doubled up with other people due to loss of housing or economic hardship Hours: Varies by school district Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides basic needs, essential support services, crisis intervention, and advocacy. Services include a drop-in center, food pantry, children's clothing, school supplies, personal grooming supplies, feminine hygiene supplies, mentoring, information and referrals for other assistance agencies, and assistance in applying for identification. 817 S 10th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 and their families who are experiencing homelessness Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 1pm-5pm Summer: Tuesday-Wednesday 1pm-5pm (541) 267-3104 7115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY TRANSITIONAL SCHOOL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A private school dedicated to serving children whose families are experiencing homelessness, housing transitions, or ongoing poverty-related crises. The school provides a stable learning environment with an individualized academic curriculum, breakfast, lunch, and snacks, medical and dental services and referrals, as well as clothing, shoes, basic needs items, and transportation to support consistent school attendance for children in transition. 6601 NE Killingsworth Street Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Grades Pre-K–8 for children whose families are in transition, experiencing homelessness, or for unaccompanied youth. Homeless children and youth are individuals who lack a fixed, regular, and adequate nighttime residence. This includes children and youth who share housing with others due to loss of housing or economic hardship, live in motels, parks, or campgrounds, have a primary nighttime residence that is not designed for sleeping, live in cars, abandoned buildings, substandard housing, or similar situations, or are migratory children living in circumstances like those described above. Unaccompanied youth are young people not in the physical custody of a parent or guardian who live on their own in any of the homeless situations described in the McKinney-Vento Homeless Education Act. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 249-8582 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN BENTON LINCOLN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT LINCOLN COUNTY HOMELESS EDUCATION LITERACY PROJECT HOMELESS EDUCATION AND LITERACY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Homeless Education and Literacy Project (HELP) is a program designed to help homeless students overcome barriers to school attendance and academic success. Four HELP Centers provide resources and educational programs to students and their families in the north, south, east, and west regions of Lincoln County. The HELP centers have basic needs resources and enrichment programs that promote literacy and education. 459 SW Coast Highway Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Students who lack a fixed, regular, and adequate nighttime residence. The HELP Advocate will determine eligibility. Qualifying living situations can include emergency/transitional shelter, motel/hotel, car, park, campground, RV, abandoned building, unsuitable living conditions, and shared housing due to economic hardship. Youth who have run away or are homeless on their own are also eligible. Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm, follows the school district calendar Email (541) 265-4506 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND PUBLIC SCHOOLS PPS MCKINNEY-VENTO RESOURCE PAGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Portland Public School's McKinney Vento resource website provides a referral form for the McKinney-Vento program and information about other social and health resources for houseless families. Assistance through the McKinney-Vento program for qualifying households may include: -Enrollment support -Assistance with academic fees -Feasible transportation to school of origin (school attended when student qualified for Title X) -Free meals at school -School clothes and supplies -Referrals to health and dental care -Referrals to rental and housing assistance 501 N Dixon Street Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Students who are at risk of becoming homeless or who are currently homeless. The definition of homelessness includes students that: -Lack a regular, fixed, and adequate nighttime residence (substandard housing) -Are sharing housing due to economic struggles (doubled-up) -Are living in a shelter, hotel, or motel -Are living in a public place not designed for sleeping (cars, parks) -Are unaccompanied (not living with a parent or guardian) -Are awaiting foster care placement -Were abandoned in a hospital Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 916-5770 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION MCKINNEY-VENTO HOMELESS EDUCATION ASSISTANCE ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY COMMUNITY TRANSITIONAL SCHOOL COMMUNITY TRANSITIONAL SCHOOL LINN BENTON LINCOLN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT HOMELESS EDUCATION AND LITERACY PROJECT PORTLAND PUBLIC SCHOOLS PPS MCKINNEY-VENTO RESOURCE PAGE
